title: Define per-symbol metadata schema + originality/license policy
context: Every symbol in open-pictograms must carry a machine-readable metadata record — covering concept identity, category, multilingual labels, comprehension scores, cultural-review status, safety class, license, and provenance — so that downstream tools (build pipeline, catalog site, OBF exporter, validation harness) can operate without manual intervention. An originality/license policy must accompany the schema so contributors know exactly what visual references are permitted and how to record an originality attestation.
objective: Author and publish the per-symbol metadata schema (JSON Schema or equivalent) and the accompanying originality/license policy that all draw/batch tasks will follow when creating and submitting symbol metadata records.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema covers all required fields: conceptId, category, interopRefs, labels (with reviewer provenance), svgSource, variants, styleConformance, safetyClass, referencedStandard, comprehensionScore, culturalReview, license, attribution, provenance, disclaimer, and status.
Schema is machine-readable (JSON Schema draft-07 or later) and validated by the build pipeline; an invalid metadata record fails the build.
Originality/license policy is co-published: permitted references (AIGA/DOT PD) named; NC/SA and proprietary sources explicitly excluded; originality attestation field required in provenance.
Policy states the required attribution string format and how it must travel with every export bundle.
Schema and policy are versioned, published in the repo (metadata/schema.json + docs), and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
resources: Open Board Format (OBF) — open AAC board interchange format
output: metadata/schema.json (machine-readable per-symbol metadata schema) and metadata/LICENSE-POLICY.md (originality/license policy with attribution requirements), both versioned and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
